Plaintiff S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Depositions And Production Of Documents With Request For Sanctions Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-13-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Joshua Safran, Esq. (510.576.6766; jsafran@rudderlawgroup.com) of Rudder Law Group LLP for Plaintiff.
Robert Weiss, Esq. (415.655.9753; robert@msllp.law) of McLaughlin Sanchez LLP for Defendants.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF LYMPHOMA SUPPORT GROUP'S Motion To Compel Defendants' Depositions And Production Of Documents With Request For Sanctions:

Motion to compel GRANTED. The deposition notices for the named Defendants were properly served and Defendants did not timely move for a protective order or serve objections, which is sufficient to grant the motion. Depositions must be completed within 15 days of notice of entry unless otherwise agreed by the parties. Sanctions are DENIED. While Defendants should have served objections or filed motions for protective orders earlier, the meet and confer correspondence demonstrates efforts to schedule the depositions. Considering these discussions, Defendants were substantially justified even though I conclude they should have served formal objections to preserve their objections.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
